title: 顺水推舟
summary:
During an interrogation, Francis Claud denies involvement in bribing Major Potter to falsely identify the deceased as Robert Andrich (also known as André Hercule). Francis admits to wanting a share of Gordon's money through unconventional means but denies fabricating evidence to strip Rosalind of her status. Poirot informs Francis that Major Potter committed suicide that afternoon. Poirot explains that Potter, facing financial hardship and the pressure of repeating his lies under oath, chose suicide rather than facing the consequences of his false testimony.

The following morning, Poirot meets with Inspector Spence. Poirot reveals that the man claiming to be Inoke Yadon is actually Charles Trenton, a known swindler with a history of using high-status appearances to facilitate theft. They discuss the dismissal of the case against David Hunter, noting that a witness, Jimmy Pierce, saw a woman in a tweed coat and an orange scarf near a phone box on the night in question. Poirot suggests that the woman's appearance—including heavy makeup and slacks—might be a disguise used by a local resident, such as Lynn Marchmont or Madame Jeremy, to avoid recognition.

Poirot concludes that the case is fundamentally flawed because the deceased's character does not match that of André Hercule. He notes that the man in Stoke was a blackmailer, whereas André was an eccentric, chivalrous man. Poirot expresses concern regarding the behavior of David Hunter and Rosalind Claud, noting that Rosalind appears to be afraid not just for her property, but for her life.
